usage_notes: Feminine noun; room for cooking and preparing food
common_collocations: dans la cuisine, la cuisine française, équipée
image_context: A modern well-equipped kitchen with countertops and appliances
example_1: Je prépare le repas dans la cuisine.
example_1_translation: I prepare the meal in the kitchen.
example_2: La cuisine est grande et lumineuse.
example_2_translation: The kitchen is large and bright.
example_3: Nous cuisinons ensemble dans la cuisine.
example_3_translation: We cook together in the kitchen.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'kwee-zeen'; the 'u' is pronounced with lips rounded
memory_hook: Cuisine = cooking, the heart of home
related_words: la maison, la nourriture, le four, le réfrigérateur
common_mistakes: Cuisine is feminine (la cuisine), not masculine

usage_notes: Feminine compound noun; phrase for bathroom
common_collocations: dans la salle de bain, la salle de bain privée
image_search_query: la salle de bain douche lavabo
image_context: A clean white bathroom with a sink and bathtub
example_1: Je me lave les mains dans la salle de bain.
example_1_translation: I wash my hands in the bathroom.
example_2: La salle de bain est à côté de la chambre.
example_2_translation: The bathroom is next to the bedroom.
example_3: Il y a une douche dans la salle de bain.
example_3_translation: There is a shower in the bathroom.
pronunciation_tips: Sal = sal (room), bain = bath. Sounds like 'sal duh ben'
memory_hook: Salle de bain = hall of bath - where you bathe
related_words: la toilette, la douche, le lavabo, le miroir
common_mistakes: It's a phrase (salle de bain), not just one word

usage_notes: Masculine noun; room where family sits together
common_collocations: dans le salon, le canapé du salon, le salon français
image_context: A cozy living room with a sofa and coffee table
example_1: Nous regardons la télévision dans le salon.
example_1_translation: We watch television in the living room.
example_2: Le salon a un grand canapé.
example_2_translation: The living room has a large sofa.
example_3: Les invités attendent dans le salon.
example_3_translation: The guests are waiting in the living room.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'sal-on'; the 'on' at the end is nasal
memory_hook: Salon = where you relax and sit together
related_words: la maison, le canapé, le fauteuil, la télévision
common_mistakes: Salon is masculine (le salon), not feminine

common_collocations: dans la chambre, une belle chambre, la chambre à coucher
image_context: A peaceful bedroom with a bed and nightstand
example_2: La chambre est calme et silencieuse.
example_2_translation: The bedroom is calm and quiet.
example_3: Ma chambre a un grand lit.
example_3_translation: My bedroom has a large bed.
memory_hook: Chambre = chamber, your private sleeping space
related_words: le lit, la maison, l'armoire, la fenêtre
common_mistakes: Chambre is feminine (la chambre), not masculine

common_collocations: un couteau aiguisé, couper avec le couteau, un couteau tranchant
image_context: A sharp kitchen knife on a wooden cutting board
example_1: J'utilise le couteau pour couper le pain.
example_1_translation: I use the knife to cut the bread.
example_2: Le couteau est tranchant, fait attention.
example_2_translation: The knife is sharp, be careful.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'koo-toe'; both syllables are clear
memory_hook: Couteau = cuts meat - a cutting tool
related_words: l'assiette, la fourchette, l'aliment, le repas
common_mistakes: Couteau is masculine (le couteau), not feminine

common_collocations: une cuillère à soupe, une cuillère à café, remuer avec la cuillère
image_context: A silver spoon resting beside a bowl of soup
example_1: Je mélange la soupe avec une cuillère.
example_1_translation: I stir the soup with a spoon.
example_2: Il faut une cuillère pour le café.
example_2_translation: We need a spoon for the coffee.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'kwee-yair'; the 'ill' makes a 'y' sound
memory_hook: Cuillère = scoop the soup - a scooping tool
related_words: le couteau, la fourchette, le bol, la soupe
common_mistakes: Cuillère is feminine (la cuillère), not masculine

usage_notes: Feminine noun; prong utensil for eating
common_collocations: une fourchette à gauche, manger avec la fourchette, une fourchette et un couteau
image_context: A metal fork on a dinner plate ready for a meal
example_1: Tu as besoin d'une fourchette pour manger.
example_1_translation: You need a fork to eat.
example_2: La fourchette est à gauche de l'assiette.
example_2_translation: The fork is to the left of the plate.
memory_hook: Fourchette = four-chette, has prongs for eating
related_words: le couteau, la cuillère, l'assiette, la nourriture
common_mistakes: Fourchette is feminine (la fourchette), not masculine

usage_notes: Feminine noun starting with vowel; dish for serving food
common_collocations: une assiette propre, sur l'assiette, une assiette vide
image_context: A clean white plate on a dining table set for dinner
example_1_translation: The meal is on the plate.
example_2: Je lave les assiettes après le dîner.
example_2_translation: I wash the plates after dinner.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'as-yet'; starts with vowel so use 'l'
memory_hook: Assiette = assorted food on a plate
related_words: le couteau, la fourchette, la nourriture, le bol
common_mistakes: Assiette is feminine; uses 'l'' before vowel, not 'la'

common_collocations: dans le lit, aller au lit, faire le lit
image_context: A neatly made bed with pillows and a soft blanket
example_1: Je me lève du lit à sept heures.
example_1_translation: I get up from bed at seven o'clock.
example_2: Le lit est confortable et doux.
example_2_translation: The bed is comfortable and soft.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'lee'; very short vowel sound
memory_hook: Lit = lie down on a bed
related_words: la chambre, dormir, l'oreiller, la couverture
common_mistakes: Lit is masculine (le lit), not feminine

usage_notes: Masculine noun; container for carrying items
common_collocations: un sac à dos, un sac à main, le sac d'école
synonyms: le sac à dos, la besace
image_context: A student backpack sitting on a school desk
example_1: Je mets mes livres dans mon sac.
example_1_translation: I put my books in my bag.
example_3: Mon sac de classe est bleu.
memory_hook: Sac = sack, a bag for carrying things
related_words: le sac à dos, les livres, l'école, la poche
common_mistakes: Sac is masculine (le sac), not feminine

usage_notes: Feminine noun; timepiece worn on wrist
common_collocations: regarder la montre, la montre du poignet, une belle montre
image_context: An elegant wristwatch with a leather strap
example_1: Je regarde ma montre pour l'heure.
example_1_translation: I look at my watch for the time.
example_2: Ma montre est belle et neuve.
example_2_translation: My watch is beautiful and new.
example_3_translation: What time does your watch show?
memory_hook: Montre = shows the time - a timepiece
related_words: l'heure, le temps, l'horloge, le poignet
common_mistakes: Montre is feminine (la montre), not masculine

common_collocations: manger du pain, manger de la viande, bien manger
image_context: A person eating a healthy meal at a dining table
example_1: Je mange des fruits chaque jour.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'mon-zhay'; the 'g' before 'e' sounds like 'zh'
memory_hook: Manger = man-zhay, the sound of eating
related_words: la nourriture, le repas, la bouche, le pain

common_collocations: boire de l'eau, boire du lait, boire du vin
image_context: A glass of clear water being lifted to drink
example_1: Je bois de l'eau quand j'ai soif.
example_1_translation: I drink water when I am thirsty.
example_2: Elle boit du café chaque matin.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'bwar'; the 'oi' is pronounced as one sound
memory_hook: Boire = bwah, the sound of swallowing a drink
related_words: l'eau, le lait, le jus, la soif
common_mistakes: Boire is irregular; past tense is 'bu', not 'boired'

common_collocations: bien dormir, dormir huit heures, s'endormir
image_context: A person sleeping peacefully in a cozy bed at night
example_1: J'aime dormir huit heures par nuit.
example_1_translation: I like to sleep eight hours per night.
example_2: Les enfants dorment à neuf heures.
example_2_translation: The children sleep at nine o'clock.
memory_hook: Dormir = door-meer, sleep closes the door on the day
related_words: le lit, la chambre, la nuit, se réveiller
common_mistakes: Dormir is irregular; past participle is 'dormi'

usage_notes: Regular verb; common in everyday speech
common_collocations: marcher vite, marcher lentement, marcher dans la rue
image_context: A person walking down a cobblestone street with shops
example_1: Je marche au parc chaque matin.
example_1_translation: I walk to the park every morning.
example_2: Il faut marcher dans la rue.
example_2_translation: We need to walk in the street.
example_3_translation: Let's walk together toward the station.
memory_hook: Marcher = march-ay, the sound of marching feet
related_words: le pied, la rue, aller, la promenade
